chunks.title: What INN-SIGHT is
chunks.text: INN-SIGHT is an AI consultant for hospitality design: pick a real map site, compare Option A vs B (structure + HVAC), run year stress, and get an investor memo with sourced kWh, cost, and CO2 from a deterministic model—not invented chat numbers.

chunks.title: How year stress works
chunks.text: In INN-SIGHT, stress testing means Run year stress: the engine runs your building through five extreme 48-hour weather weekends (heat-wave, summer shoulder, typical July, typical winter, deep cold)—not a full 8760-hour year. Temps prefer Open-Meteo ERA5 for your map pin (Toronto fallback if offline). You get peak kW and STABLE/ELEVATED/CRITICAL strain for Option A and B, then an investor memo.

chunks.title: Option A vs Option B
chunks.text: Option A is typically concrete + central gas HVAC. Option B is typically mass timber + heat pumps. After year stress, the memo recommends the greener option when carbon abatement cost is at or below about $170/tCO2e (Canada federal benchmark); otherwise it may keep the cheaper path. Ask after you have a memo for your actual numbers.

chunks.title: Parcel acres and land use
chunks.text: Acres and land-use (e.g. parking, brownfield) come from the selected OpenStreetMap empty-parcel polygon—an approximate footprint, not a legal survey or vacant-lot registry.

chunks.title: Live site climate
chunks.text: Site climate (temp, humidity, wind, elevation) is a live Open-Meteo forecast for the active pin. That is separate from the historical ERA5 curves used inside year stress.

chunks.title: Investor memo
chunks.text: The investor memo is the report after year stress: A/B costs, carbon, peak strain, recommendation, reasoning, narrative, and footnotes. Generate it with Place building → Run year stress, then ask Explain this memo for your numbers.

chunks.title: Rules & Compliance Engine
chunks.text: The Rules & Compliance Engine compares your assembled 3D massing (storeys, GFA, structure, HVAC) side-by-side against a City of Toronto / Ontario pack: zoning height, setbacks, angular plane, FSI, parking demand, OBC/EMTC, and Toronto Green Standard notes. It shows your model next to the rule clause and the delta (how much over/under)—like Option A vs B in the memo—not a hard pass/fail gate. Pack values are labelled heuristics for the demo site overlay; not a zoning opinion or legal advice. Ask after year stress when the multi-agent briefing includes the compliance table.

chunks.title: Honesty ledger
chunks.text: INN-SIGHT labels estimates vs sourced benchmarks. Friction is a heuristic, not a survey. Green parcels are OSM open land, not a legal registry. Stress windows are 48h extremes, not metered bills for the exact building. Compliance overlays are site-pack heuristics, not parcel-matched legal surveys.

chunks.title: Sign-in
chunks.text: When Auth0 is on, Google sign-in syncs your profile and can store past runs. Chat cannot change auth settings.
